creator: grahamperrin@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Wolfram Schneider from comment #16)

> Since Jan 2th docs.freebsd.org is fast again (<0.2 seconds). Great!

The long FreeBSD Handbook still takes some time to load e.g. <https://share.firefox.dev/3r12osJ> however I expect this. As an entire book, it's truly long (compared to some neighbouring books). 

Top-level navigation, the three links at top right, is very fast. 

----

Without quantifying: 

* today, Bugzilla feels _much_ faster. 

Since Bugzilla feels so much faster, I visited <https://www.freebsd.org/> and found the main site qualitatively faster, too. 

<https://www.freebsd.org/cgi/man.cgi?query=rc.conf&sektion=5&manpath=FreeBSD> (a relatively long man page): around ten seconds <https://share.firefox.dev/3Ganr2n>. Fast enough for me, and I imagine that cgi stuff doesn't fall under the umbrella of this bug. 

Thanks to whoever's making the improvements :-)
